VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING:  
1. Message vs. schedule: the first 32 bits of the message (L0001) are 0x634D6A23.  W[0] (L0003-L0004) is 0110 0011 0100 1101 0110 1010 0010 0011 = 0x634D6A23, so the schedule starts correctly.  
2. Round 0 consistency:  
   • temp1 (L0075) = 0x56C5578B and d (L0069) = 0xA54FF53A.  
   • Sum 0xA54FF53A + 0x56C5578B = 0xFC154CC5 which is exactly the “new e” shown in L0080.  
   • temp2 (L0078) = 0x08909AE5;  temp1 + temp2 = 0x5F5F2700, matching “new a” in L0079.  
3. Round 6 consistency:  
   • d (L0153) = 0x10A10047, temp1 (L0159) = 0xC77DFBBD → sum = 0xD81EFC04 which equals “new e” (L0164).  
   • temp2 (L0162) = 0x6E D9 20 C9;  temp1 + temp2 = 0x36571C86, identical to “new a” (L0163).  
4. Spot-checking other small steps (e.g., S0/S1 rotations in L0070-L0071, L0412-L0414) all reproduced the stated results bit-for-bit.  
Because every sampled low-level operation (bit rotations, XORs, additions) and every derived value agree with the algorithm, there is no evidence of a single-bit divergence anywhere in the trace.
